questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: Which of the following countries border the Red Sea?

options.text: Egypt, Eritrea, Saudi Arabia, and Jordan
Sudan, Oman, Yemen, and Saudi Arabia

questions.explanation: The countries bordering the Red Sea include Egypt, Sudan, and Eritrea on the west; Saudi Arabia and Yemen on the east; and Israel and Jordan in the northeast (via the Gulf of Aqaba). Ethiopia is landlocked and does not border the Red Sea. Oman borders the Arabian Sea, and Iraq borders the Persian Gulf.
questions.takeaway: The Red Sea is bordered by six primary nations, with Israel and Jordan accessing it through the narrow Gulf of Aqaba.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Bordering Nations: West: Egypt, Sudan and Eritrea. North East: Israel & Jordan via the gulf of Aqaba. East: Saudi Arabia & Yemen.
